summary: Full backend (40/40 pytest passing) and frontend e2e testing of Dentalin Turkish dental clinic site. All public endpoints return correctly seeded data, JWT auth + admin CRUD work, appointment creates a lead, quiz rule-based recommender returns correct slugs. Frontend: HomePage renders all sections, hero CTAs, appointment 3-step flow successfully creates a 'Talebiniz Alındı' confirmation, quiz dialog returns recommendation, admin login redirects to dashboard with full sidebar nav, logout clears token. Mobile bottom nav + floating WhatsApp/Call appear on 390px viewport.

minor.issue: allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False means httpOnly access_token cookie is never sent in cross-site browser requests. Frontend Bearer-token via localStorage works, but cookie-based auth path (advertised in playbook) is effectively dead cross-origin.
No PATCH endpoint defined (only POST/GET/DELETE) while doctors/treatments/testimonials/faqs all support PATCH. Admin UI cannot edit a before/after item without delete+recreate.

issues: data-testid uses raw Turkish labels with spaces/diacritics (e.g., 'admin-nav-Potansiyel Müşteriler', 'admin-nav-Hasta Yorumları', 'admin-nav-Galeri (Öncesi/Sonrası)'). Spec asked for kebab-case slugs. Recommend slugifying labels for testids to make automation robust.

action_items: Add PATCH /api/admin/before-after/{id} endpoint for edit support.
Either set CORS allow_credentials=True with explicit origins or remove the misleading set_cookie on login (Bearer token already works).
Slugify admin sidebar data-testid values (e.g., admin-nav-leads, admin-nav-testimonials, admin-nav-before-after, admin-nav-faqs) instead of using raw Turkish labels with spaces.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 800+ lines and would benefit from being split into routers (auth.py, public.py, admin.py) and models.py. Currently borderline acceptable.

Login sets cookie with samesite=none, secure=true but CORS has allow_credentials=False — cookie is set on the response but cannot be read by the browser for subsequent cross-site fetches. Inconsistent.

Quiz endpoint stores a lead only when name OR phone is supplied; in HomePage the optional contact step is skippable, which means anonymous quiz completions silently produce no lead. This is by design but worth confirming with product.
Seed runs on every startup; idempotent guards exist (count_documents == 0). Admin password is rotated to match .env if mismatched, which is acceptable for this environment.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows (home render, appointment 3-step submit, quiz recommendation, admin login/dashboard/logout, mobile bottom nav, floating buttons)

seed_data_creation: Backend startup seeds 6 treatments (4 featured), 4 doctors, 6 testimonials, 4 before-after, 6 FAQs, 1 admin user. Tests also create several TEST_-prefixed appointments/leads/doctors/treatments/etc., the CRUD tests clean up after themselves; the TEST_ appointments+leads are intentionally left to verify the appointment→lead pipeline.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all 40 critical API paths and can be re-run via 'pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v'. Admin sidebar testids use raw Turkish labels (with spaces) — if you automate sidebar clicks use the URL pattern instead (e.g., click('a[href="/admin/leads"]')). Quiz recommendation screen heading reads 'Size En Uygun Tedavi' not 'Önerilen Tedavi'.
rca of the issue: No bugs blocking flows. Two non-blocking findings: (1) PATCH missing on /api/admin/before-after — main agent should add this endpoint for edit parity; (2) Cookie auth is half-wired due to CORS allow_credentials=False — frontend currently uses Bearer token from localStorage so end-user experience works.
